projectDescription: A Python toolkit for simulating the thermal evolution and carbon emissions of magmatic sill intrusions in two dimensions, designed for geoscientists studying Large Igneous Provinces.
keyFeatures: 2D heat diffusion simulation with multiple solver methods (ADI, convolution, implicit)
Stochastic sill swarm emplacement with configurable flux, volume, depth, and geometry
SILLi (Easy%Ro vitrinite reflectance) carbon emissions model
Sillburp multi-kerogen kinetics carbon emissions model
Parallel simulation runs via joblib across flux/volume parameter space
VTK output compatible with ParaView for 3D visualization
Sill thermal interaction network analysis via NetworkX KD-Tree proximity graphs
Latent heat and radiogenic heat source terms
publicApiSurface: cool.diff_solve(k, a, b, dx, dy, dt, Tnow, q, method, H, k_const)
cool.conv_smooth_solve_adi(k, a, b, dx, dy, dt, Tnow, q, Af, H)
emit.SILLi_emissions(T_field, density, lithology, porosity, TOC_prev, dt, ...)
emit.sillburp(T_field, TOC_prev, density, lithology, porosity, dt, ...)
rules.mult_sill(T_field, majr, minr, height, x_space, dx, dy, rock, ...)
rules.sill_3Dcube(x, y, z, dx, dy, n_sills, ...)
